creation_time.description: The timestamp when the compute cluster was created.

description.description: The description of the compute cluster.

id.description: A unique identifier for the compute cluster.

name.description: The name of the compute cluster.

provisioning_status.description: The provisioning status of the compute cluster.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region where the compute cluster is provisioned.

ssh_private_key.description: The SSH private key for accessing the compute cluster.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the compute cluster.

workload_pools.description: A list of pools of workload nodes in the compute cluster.

enable_public_ip.description: Whether to assign a public IP address to each VM in this workload pool.

firewall_rules.description: A list of firewall rules applied to the VMs in this workload pool.

direction.description: The direction of the traffic to which this firewall rule applies.

ports.description: The ports to which this firewall rule applies. This can be a single port, or a range of ports.

prefixes.description: A set of CIDR prefixes to which this firewall rule applies.

protocol.description: The IP protocol to which this firewall rule applies.

flavor_id.description: The identifier of the flavor (machine type) used for the workload pool VMs.

image_id.description: The identifier of the image used for initializing the boot disk of the workload pool VMs.

machines.description: A list of machines in this workload pool.

private_ip.description: The private IP address of the machine.

public_ip.description: The public IP address of the machine, if assigned.

name.description: The name of the workload pool.

replicas.description: The number of replicas (VMs) to provision in this workload pool.

user_data.description: The data to pass to the VMs at boot time.

capacity.description: The total capacity of the file storage, in gibibytes.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the file storage was created.

default_snapshot_protection_enabled.description: Whether platform-managed Default Snapshot Protection is enabled for the file storage. This is separate from any user-managed snapshot policies.

description.description: The description of the file storage.

id.description: A unique identifier for the file storage.

name.description: The name of the file storage.

project_id.description: The identifier of the project where the file storage is provisioned.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region where the file storage is provisioned.

root_squash.description: Indicates whether root squashing is enabled for the file storage.

size.description: The amount of storage currently used, in gibibytes.

snapshot_policies.description: The user-managed snapshot policies for the file storage, identified by `name`.

name.description: The snapshot policy name.

retention.description: How many snapshots this policy retains.

keep.description: The number of snapshots to retain.

schedule.description: When snapshots are taken for this policy.

day_of_month.description: The day of month snapshots are taken (1 through 28).

day_of_week.description: The day of week snapshots are taken (`monday` through `sunday`).

time_of_day.description: The UTC time of day snapshots are taken, in `HH:MMZ` form.

storage_class_id.description: The identifier of the storage class assigned to the file storage.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the file storage.

id.description: The identifier of the network to which the file storage is attached.

mount_source.description: The network path for mounting the file storage.

block.description: The network to which the file storage is attached.

description.description: The description of the file storage class.

id.description: A unique identifier for the file storage class.

name.description: The name of the file storage class.

protocols.description: A list of protocols supported by the file storage class.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region where the file storage class is available. If not specified, this defaults to the region ID configured in the provider.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the group was created.

description.description: The description of the group.

id.description: A unique identifier for the group.

name.description: The name of the group.

provisioning_status.description: The provisioning status of the group.

role_ids.description: The set of role identifiers granted to members of this group.

service_account_ids.description: The set of service account identifiers that are members of this group.

subjects.description: The set of federated identity subjects that are members of this group.

email.description: The email address for the subject, when supplied by the issuer.

id.description: The subject identifier issued by the issuer.

issuer.description: The OIDC issuer URL that asserts this subject.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the group.

user_ids.description: The set of user identifiers that are members of this group.

description.description: The description of the project.

description.description: The description of the instance.

flavor_id.description: The identifier of the flavor used for the instance.

id.description: A unique identifier for the instance.

image_id.description: The identifier of the image used for the instance.

name.description: The name of the instance.

power_state.description: The power state of the instance.

private_ip.description: The private IP address assigned to the instance.

project_id.description: The identifier of the project where the instance is provisioned.

public_ip.description: The public IP address assigned to the instance.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region where the instance is provisioned.

ssh_certificate_authority_id.description: The identifier of the SSH certificate authority used to bootstrap login trust when the backing server is created.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the instance.

user_data.description: The data to pass to the instance at boot time.

allowed_destinations.description: A list of CIDR blocks that are allowed to egress from the instance without SNAT.

enable_public_ip.description: Indicates whether the instance has a public IP.

network_id.description: The identifier of the network where the instance is provisioned.

security_group_ids.description: A list of security group identifiers associated with the instance.

block.description: The network interface configuration of the instance.

cpus.description: The number of CPUs allocated to the instance flavor.

description.description: The description of the instance flavor.

disk_size.description: The disk storage allocated to the instance flavor, in gigabytes.

id.description: A unique identifier for the instance flavor.

memory_size.description: The memory allocated to the instance flavor, in gibibytes.

name.description: The name of the instance flavor.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region where the instance flavor is available. If not specified, this defaults to the region ID configured in the provider.

logical_count.description: The total number of logical GPU units available for use.

memory_size.description: The memory available on the GPU, in gibibytes.

model.description: The model name of the GPU.

physical_count.description: The number of physical GPU devices available in the instance flavor.

block.description: The GPU configuration for the instance flavor, if available.

instance_id.description: The identifier of the instance associated with the SSH key.

private_key.description: The private SSH key for accessing the instance.

cidr_block.description: The CIDR block assigned to the network.

routes.description: A list of routes associated with the network.

destination.description: The destination CIDR block for the route.

nexthop.description: The next-hop address for the route.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the network.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the access key was created.

description.description: The description of the access key.

endpoint_id.description: The identifier of the object storage endpoint this access key belongs to.

id.description: A unique identifier for the access key.

identity_policy.description: The identity policy name the access key is bound to.

name.description: The name of the access key.

project_id.description: The identifier of the project the access key belongs to.

block.description: Retrieves information about an existing object storage access key. The S3 secret is intentionally not exposed by this data source — it is only available at creation time. Use the resource (and protect Terraform state) to manage it.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the object storage endpoint was created.

description.description: The description of the object storage endpoint.

endpoint_class_id.description: The identifier of the endpoint class the endpoint was created with.

exposure.description: The externally reachable endpoints for the object storage endpoint.

public.description: Connection details for the public exposure mode.

dns_name.description: The DNS hostname clients use to reach the endpoint over the public network.

id.description: A unique identifier for the object storage endpoint.

identity_policies.description: Identity policies configured on the endpoint.

document.description: The identity policy document encoded as a JSON object string.

name.description: The identity policy name.

project_id.description: The identifier of the project where the object storage endpoint is provisioned.

block.description: Retrieves information about an existing object storage endpoint by its unique identifier.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the endpoint class was created.

description.description: The description of the endpoint class.

id.description: A unique identifier for the endpoint class.

name.description: The name of the endpoint class.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region where the endpoint class is available. If not specified, this defaults to the region ID configured in the provider.

supported_endpoint_types.description: Endpoint exposure types supported by this class. Possible values are `public` and `private`.

block.description: Retrieves an object storage endpoint class by its unique identifier. Endpoint classes determine an endpoint's exposure type (public/private) and regional availability.

constraints.description: The scheduling policy applied when selecting hosts from the reservation.

max_skew.description: The maximum difference in host count between any two domains.

min_domains.description: The minimum number of domains that must receive at least one selected host.

when_unsatisfiable.description: The behaviour when the constraint cannot be fully satisfied.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the placement was created.

description.description: The description of the placement.

host_count.description: The number of hosts allocated from the reservation.

id.description: A unique identifier for the placement.

name.description: The name of the placement.

network_id.description: The identifier of the network the placement belongs to.

project_id.description: The identifier of the project the placement is provisioned in.

provisioning_status.description: The provisioning status of the placement.

ready_host_count.description: The number of hosts whose Region server resources are ready.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region the placement belongs to.

reservation_id.description: The identifier of the reservation the placement allocates from.

server_spec.description: Region server options applied to each pinned server.

image_id.description: The image used for each pinned server.

networking.description: Region server networking options applied to each pinned server.

allowed_source_addresses.description: A list of network prefixes that are allowed to egress from each server.

enable_public_ip.description: Whether or not a public IP is provisioned for each server.

security_group_ids.description: A list of security group IDs applied to each server.

user_data.description: Base64-encoded configuration information or scripts used upon launch.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the placement.

description.description: The description of the region.

id.description: A unique identifier for the region.

name.description: The name of the region.

accelerator.description: The public accelerator model or family reserved.

claimed_unit_count.description: The number of reservation units successfully claimed.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the reservation was created.

description.description: The description of the reservation.

id.description: A unique identifier for the reservation.

machine_flavor_id.description: The resolved Region machine flavor used for pinned servers.

name.description: The name of the reservation.

project_id.description: The identifier of the project the reservation is provisioned in.

provisioning_status.description: The provisioning status of the reservation.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region the capacity is reserved in.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the reservation.

topology_hash.description: A hash of the claimed topology projection accepted by the reservation.

topology_observed_at.description: The timestamp when the claimed topology projection was last observed.

unit_count.description: The number of reservation units reserved.

from_port.description: The starting port of the port range for the security group rule.

protocol.description: The protocol for the security group rule.

to_port.description: The ending port of the port range for the security group rule.

type.description: The type of the security group rule.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the SSH certificate authority was created.

description.description: The description of the SSH certificate authority.

name.description: The name of the SSH certificate authority.

public_key.description: The SSH CA public key in OpenSSH format.

compute_service_api_endpoint.description: The endpoint of the Nscale Compute Service API server.

identity_service_api_endpoint.description: The endpoint of the Nscale Identity Service API server.

organization_id.description: The identifier of the organization for which resources are managed.

project_id.description: The default project identifier for project-scoped resources that do not set their own project_id. Optional: org-level workflows and configurations that set project_id on every resource do not need it.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region for which resources are managed. Regional resources include a top-level region_id field, allowing the region to be explicitly specified and to override the default region when provided.

region_service_api_endpoint.description: The endpoint of the Nscale Region Service API server.

reservation_service_api_endpoint.description: The endpoint of the Nscale Reservation Service API server.

service_token.description: The service token for authenticating with the Nscale API server.

storage_service_api_endpoint.description: The endpoint of the Nscale Storage Service API server.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the compute cluster was created.

description.description: The description of the compute cluster.

id.description: A unique identifier for the compute cluster.

name.description: The name of the compute cluster.

provisioning_status.description: The provisioning status of the compute cluster.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region where the compute cluster is provisioned. If not specified, this defaults to the region ID configured in the provider.

ssh_private_key.description: The SSH private key for accessing the compute cluster.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the compute cluster.

workload_pools.description: A list of pools of workload nodes in the compute cluster.

allowed_address_pairs.description: Allowed addresses that can pass through this workload pool's network ports. Each pair specifies a CIDR prefix and optionally a MAC address. Typically required when the machine is operating as a router.

mac_address.description: The MAC address to allow. Optional.

enable_public_ip.description: Whether to assign a public IP address to each VM in this workload pool. Default is `true`.

firewall_rules.description: A list of firewall rules for the VMs in this workload pool.

direction.description: The direction of the traffic to which this firewall rule applies. Default is `ingress`.

ports.description: The ports to which this firewall rule applies. This can be a single port, or a range of ports. For example: `22`, `80-443`.

prefixes.description: A set of CIDR prefixes to which this firewall rule applies.

protocol.description: The IP protocol to which this firewall rule applies. Valid values are `tcp` or `udp`.

flavor_id.description: The identifier of the flavor (machine type) used for the workload pool VMs.

image_id.description: The identifier of the image used for initializing the boot disk of the workload pool VMs.

machines.description: A list of machines in this workload pool.

private_ip.description: The private IP address of the machine.

public_ip.description: The public IP address of the machine, if assigned.

name.description: The name of the workload pool.

replicas.description: The number of replicas (VMs) to provision in this workload pool.

user_data.description: The data to pass to the VMs at boot time.

create.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

delete.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.

update.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

capacity.description: The total capacity requested for the file storage, in gibibytes.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the file storage was created.

default_snapshot_protection_enabled.description: Whether platform-managed Default Snapshot Protection is enabled for the file storage. This is separate from any user-managed snapshot policies. When omitted or null, the platform default applies and Terraform reads back the resolved value without enforcing it; when set to `true` or `false`, Terraform manages the setting and drift-corrects out-of-band changes.

description.description: The description of the file storage.

id.description: A unique identifier for the file storage.

name.description: The name of the file storage.

project_id.description: The identifier of the project where the file storage is provisioned. If not specified, this defaults to the project ID configured in the provider.

refresh_usage.description: Whether to refresh the computed `size` usage value from the Nscale API. Set to `false` to keep `size` stable in Terraform state and avoid plan noise from file usage changes.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region where the file storage is provisioned. If not specified, this defaults to the region ID configured in the provider.

root_squash.description: Whether root squashing is applied to the file storage to restrict root access for clients.

size.description: The amount of storage currently used, in gibibytes.

snapshot_policies.description: The user-managed snapshot policies for the file storage. These are separate from platform-managed Default Snapshot Protection, which is never represented here. When omitted or null, Terraform observes and preserves whatever policies exist remotely; when set to an empty set (`[]`), Terraform enforces that no user-managed policies exist; when set to one or more policies, Terraform enforces exactly that set. Policies are identified by `name` and ordering is not significant. At most four policies are allowed.

name.description: The snapshot policy name. Acts as the policy's stable identity key and must be unique within the file storage.

retention.description: How many snapshots this policy retains.

keep.description: The number of snapshots to retain.

schedule.description: When snapshots are taken for this policy.

day_of_month.description: The UTC day of month snapshots are taken (1 through 28). Applies to monthly schedules.

day_of_week.description: The UTC day of week snapshots are taken (`monday` through `sunday`). Applies to weekly schedules.

time_of_day.description: The UTC time of day snapshots are taken, in `HH:MMZ` form. Applies to daily, weekly, and monthly schedules.

storage_class_id.description: The identifier of the storage class used for the file storage.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the file storage.

id.description: The unique identifier of the network to attach the file storage to.

mount_source.description: The network path used to mount the file storage.

block.description: The network to which the file storage is attached.

create.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

delete.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.

update.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

id.description: A unique identifier for the group.

name.description: The name of the group.

provisioning_status.description: The provisioning status of the group.

role_ids.description: The set of role identifiers granted to members of this group. Roles are pre-configured platform resources referenced by their identifier.

service_account_ids.description: The set of service account identifiers that are members of this group.

subjects.description: The set of identity subjects that are members of this group. This is read-only: the identity service derives it from `user_ids` (each member user produces a subject) and any federated identities. Manage membership through `user_ids`, not this attribute.

email.description: The email address for the subject, when supplied by the issuer.

id.description: The subject identifier issued by the issuer.

issuer.description: The OIDC issuer URL that asserts this subject.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the group.

user_ids.description: The set of user identifiers that are members of this group. Users are provisioned by the external identity platform and referenced here by their identifier.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the access key was created.

description.description: The description of the access key.

endpoint_id.description: The identifier of the object storage endpoint this access key belongs to. The endpoint is immutable for an access key — changing it forces replacement.

id.description: A unique identifier for the access key.

identity_policy.description: The name of an identity policy configured on the parent endpoint that this key is bound to.

name.description: The name of the access key.

project_id.description: The identifier of the project where the access key is provisioned. If not specified, this defaults to the project ID configured in the provider.

secret.description: The S3 secret access key. Returned only when the access key is created and never re-read from the API. Treat as write-once: store it in a secret manager, or replace the resource (`terraform apply -replace=...`) to obtain a new value.

create.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

delete.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.

block.description: An S3-compatible access key bound to an object storage endpoint. The access key's secret is returned only at creation; protect Terraform state accordingly. The resource is immutable — any change forces replacement, which generates a fresh secret.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the object storage endpoint was created.

description.description: The description of the object storage endpoint.

endpoint_class_id.description: The identifier of the endpoint class that determines the endpoint's exposure type and regional availability. The endpoint class is immutable after creation.

exposure.description: The externally reachable endpoints for the object storage endpoint, populated once provisioning completes.

public.description: Connection details for the public exposure mode, when the endpoint class supports it.

dns_name.description: The DNS hostname clients use to reach the endpoint over the public network.

id.description: A unique identifier for the object storage endpoint.

identity_policies.description: Identity policies configured on the endpoint. Policy names must be unique within the endpoint. Updating this attribute replaces the full set of configured policies.

document.description: A provider-specific identity policy document encoded as a JSON object string. Use Terraform's `jsonencode()` to construct it. Whitespace and key ordering are normalised so equivalent documents do not produce diffs.

name.description: The identity policy name. Must be unique within this endpoint.
The name of the object storage endpoint.

project_id.description: The identifier of the project where the object storage endpoint is provisioned. If not specified, this defaults to the project ID configured in the provider.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region where the object storage endpoint is provisioned. If not specified, this defaults to the region ID configured in the provider.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the object storage endpoint.

create.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

delete.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.

update.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

block.description: An S3-compatible object storage endpoint provisioned in an Nscale project. The endpoint exposes a chosen endpoint class's connectivity (public DNS today; private may be added in future) and carries one or more identity policies that govern what its access keys are allowed to do.

constraints.description: The scheduling policy applied when selecting hosts from the reservation. Changing this forces a new placement to be created.

max_skew.description: The maximum difference in host count between any two domains. Applicable only when `policy` is `spread`.

min_domains.description: The minimum number of domains that must receive at least one selected host. Applicable only when `policy` is `spread`. Must be less than or equal to `host_count`.

policy.description: The placement policy. `pack` fills domains sequentially, maximising locality; `spread` distributes hosts as evenly as possible across domains.

when_unsatisfiable.description: The behaviour when the constraint cannot be fully satisfied. `fail` returns an error; `bestEffort` applies the constraint as closely as it can.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the placement was created.

description.description: The description of the placement. Changing this forces a new placement to be created.

host_count.description: The number of hosts to allocate from the reservation. Must be at least 1. Changing this forces a new placement to be created.

id.description: A unique identifier for the placement.

name.description: The name of the placement. Changing this forces a new placement to be created.

network_id.description: The identifier of the network to attach hosts to. This also determines the InfiniBand partition boundary; all hosts in a placement share a single partition key. Changing this forces a new placement to be created.

project_id.description: The identifier of the project the placement is provisioned in.

provisioning_status.description: The provisioning status of the placement.

ready_host_count.description: The number of hosts whose Region server resources are ready.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region the placement belongs to.

reservation_id.description: The identifier of the reservation to allocate hosts from. Changing this forces a new placement to be created.

server_spec.description: Region server options applied to each pinned server. Changing this forces a new placement to be created.

image_id.description: The image to use for each pinned server.

networking.description: Region server networking options applied to each pinned server.

allowed_source_addresses.description: A list of network prefixes that are allowed to egress from each server. By default, only packets from the server's network interface's IP address are allowed to enter the network.

enable_public_ip.description: Whether or not to provision a public IP for each server.

security_group_ids.description: A list of security group IDs to apply to each server.

user_data.description: Base64-encoded configuration information or scripts to use upon launch.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the placement. Changing this forces a new placement to be created.

create.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

delete.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.

accelerator.description: The public accelerator model or family to reserve, for example `GB300`. Changing this forces a new reservation to be created.

claimed_unit_count.description: The number of reservation units successfully claimed.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the reservation was created.

description.description: The description of the reservation. Changing this forces a new reservation to be created.

id.description: A unique identifier for the reservation.

machine_flavor_id.description: The resolved Region machine flavor used for pinned servers.

name.description: The name of the reservation. Changing this forces a new reservation to be created.

project_id.description: The identifier of the project the reservation is provisioned in. If not specified, this defaults to the project ID configured in the provider. Changing this forces a new reservation to be created.

provisioning_status.description: The provisioning status of the reservation.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region to reserve capacity in. If not specified, this defaults to the region ID configured in the provider. Changing this forces a new reservation to be created.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the reservation. Changing this forces a new reservation to be created.

topology_hash.description: A hash of the claimed topology projection accepted by the reservation.

topology_observed_at.description: The timestamp when the claimed topology projection was last observed.

unit.description: The public reservation granularity to reserve, for example `NVL72`. Changing this forces a new reservation to be created.

unit_count.description: The number of reservation units to reserve. Must be at least 1. Changing this forces a new reservation to be created.

create.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

delete.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the security group was created.

description.description: The description of the security group.

id.description: A unique identifier for the security group.

name.description: The name of the security group.

network_id.description: The identifier of the network to which the security group is attached.

region_id.description: The identifier of the region where the security group is provisioned.

rules.description: A list of rules for the security group.

cidr_block.description: The CIDR block for the security group rule. Default is `0.0.0.0/0`, which allows traffic from any IP address.

from_port.description: The starting port of the port range for the security group rule.

protocol.description: The protocol for the security group rule. Valid values are `any`, `tcp`, `udp`, `icmp`, or `vrrp`.

to_port.description: The ending port of the port range for the security group rule.

type.description: The type of the security group rule. Valid values are `ingress` or `egress`.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the security group.

create.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

delete.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.

update.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

creation_time.description: The timestamp when the SSH certificate authority was created.

description.description: The description of the SSH certificate authority.

id.description: A unique identifier for the SSH certificate authority.

name.description: The name of the SSH certificate authority.

project_id.description: The identifier of the project where the SSH certificate authority is provisioned. If not specified, this defaults to the project ID configured in the provider.

public_key.description: The SSH CA public key in OpenSSH format (e.g. ssh-ed25519 AAAA...).

create.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

delete.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
